herb_parts.name: root
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum root, the primary medicinal part of garlic, has been used for centuries to treat various health conditions due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. The root contains compounds such as allicin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Regular consumption of garlic root has also been linked to immune system boosters and potential cancer prevention.

herb_parts.name: dried root
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum dried root, also known as dried garlic root, is a medicinal part of the plant used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The dried root is rich in compounds such as allicin, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing blood pressure, cholesterol levels, and improving cardiovascular health. Additionally, the dried root has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, due to its carminative properties.

herb_parts.name: root extract
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum root extract is a component commonly used in traditional medicine, and it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The extract has been traditionally used to aid in digestion, reduce symptoms of respiratory issues, and even support wound healing. Research has also suggested that the root extract may have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, although further studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness.

herb_parts.name: root powder
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum root powder is a key medicinal part of garlic, known for its antibacterial and antiviral properties, which help to boost the immune system and fight off infections. The powder, often used in traditional medicine, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and may help to reduce blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Additionally, it is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, promoting overall health and well-being.

herb_parts.name: rhizome
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum rhizome, commonly known as the underground stem of garlic, contains compounds with antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The rhizome of Allium sativum has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including high blood pressure, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. Its active compounds, such as allicin, have been shown to have potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

herb_parts.name: dried rhizome
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum dried rhizome is used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It is believed to help reduce pain and inflammation, as well as exhibit antioxidant and antifungal activities. Dried rhizome extracts are also used to support digestive health and immune function.

herb_parts.name: rhizome extract
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum rhizome extract, also known as garlic extract, has been widely used for its medicinal properties due to its high concentration of active compounds such as allicin and diallyl disulfide. These compounds have been shown to possess potent antimicrobial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, making garlic extract effective against various bacterial and viral infections. Additionally, garlic extract has been traditionally used to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

herb_parts.name: rhizome powder
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum rhizome powder, also known as garlic powder, is a common herbal supplement used to support cardiovascular health by reducing cholesterol levels and blood pressure. It is also used to boost the immune system and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can aid in fighting off infections. Additionally, garlic powder has been traditionally used to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties and support digestive health.

herb_parts.name: leaf
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum leaf has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaf contains compounds such as quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. These properties make the leaf a potential candidate for treating various health conditions, including respiratory infections and skin diseases.

herb_parts.name: dried leaf
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum dried leaf is used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It is believed to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. In Ayurvedic medicine, the dried leaf is also used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs.

herb_parts.name: leaf extract
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum leaf extract has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It may also exhibit antimicrobial effects, helping to protect against various infections. Additionally, the extract has been studied for its possible role in lowering cholesterol levels and improving cardiovascular health.

herb_parts.name: leaf powder
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum leaf powder is used in various traditional medicine practices due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. It has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential remedy for certain skin conditions and wounds. Furthermore, research suggests that the powder may have antiviral properties, which could aid in the treatment of viral infections.

herb_parts.name: stem
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum stem is not commonly used in herbal medicine, however, the leaves and bulb are more commonly used. However, the leaves from the stem of garlic, also known as Allium sativum, may be used in some traditional medicine for their potential anti-inflammatory properties. The use of Allium sativum stem in medicine is generally anecdotal and requires further research to confirm its potential medicinal benefits.

herb_parts.name: flower
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum flower is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and digestive issues, due to its expectorant and carminative properties. The flower of Allium sativum is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Additionally, the flower is used to treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema due to its antiseptic and antifungal properties.

herb_parts.name: dried flower
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum dried flower is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and antimicrobial effects, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The dried flower of Allium sativum has been traditionally used to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. It is also used to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections.

herb_parts.name: flower extract
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum flower extract is a lesser-known part of the plant used in traditional medicine, and it has been found to possess antibacterial and antifungal properties. However, its potential medicinal applications and benefits are still being researched and studied. Some studies suggest that the extract may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.

herb_parts.name: fruit
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum fruit, commonly known as garlic cloves, is a significant medicinal part of the plant. The fruit contains compounds like allicin, which has antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. In traditional medicine, garlic fruit has been used to reduce blood pressure, lower cholesterol levels, and boost the immune system.

herb_parts.name: seed
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum seed is not commonly used in traditional medicine, but it is known to contain saponins and flavonoids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. However, there is limited research on the specific medicinal properties of the seed. In some traditional practices, the seed is used to treat respiratory issues and as a diuretic.

herb_parts.name: dried fruit
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum dried fruit, also known as dried garlic, contains compounds like alliin and allicin, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The dried fruit of Allium sativum is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including hypertension and digestive issues. It is also used as a natural antiseptic and antifungal agent due to its high sulfur content.

herb_parts.name: dried seed
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum dried seed is used in traditional medicine for its potential to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of indigestion. The dried seed of this plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may aid in fighting off infections. Additionally, it is sometimes used as a natural expectorant to help alleviate congestion and coughs associated with respiratory issues.

herb_parts.name: flower oil
herb_parts.desc: Allium sativum flower oil, extracted from the flowers of the garlic plant, possesses antimicrobial and antifungal properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds and skin infections. The oil's potential anti-inflammatory effects have also been studied for its possible benefits in managing conditions such as arthritis.

plant_parts.name: root
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum root, also known as garlic root, has been used in traditional medicine for its antibacterial and antiviral properties. The root of garlic contains compounds like allicin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. It is also used in some cultures to aid in digestion and lower cholesterol levels.

plant_parts.name: bulb
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum bulb contains compounds like allicin, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. The bulb is also a rich source of antioxidants, including sulfur-containing amino acids and polyphenols, that help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, the bulb's antiviral and antibacterial properties have been shown to aid in wound healing and support the immune system.

plant_parts.name: leaves
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum leaves are primarily used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. They contain various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their medicinal value. In some cultures, the leaves are used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps, due to their carminative and anti-flatulent properties.

plant_parts.name: oil
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum oil, also known as garlic oil, is a medicinal extract obtained from the bulbs of the Allium sativum plant. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including high blood pressure, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The oil contains compounds such as allicin and ajoene, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and have been shown to have antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal effects.

plant_parts.name: seeds
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum seeds, which are not commonly consumed as a medicinal part, have shown potential in traditional medicine due to their antiviral and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand their medicinal benefits and potential uses. In some traditional practices, the seeds are used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and inflammation.

plant_parts.name: juice
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum juice, which is also known as garlic juice, is extracted from the cloves of the plant and has been used for its medicinal properties. The juice contains compounds such as allicin, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. It is often used to reduce blood pressure, cholesterol levels, and to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.

plant_parts.name: flowers
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum flowers are not commonly used for medicinal purposes, but they do contain some compounds that may have antibacterial properties. However, the flowers are mostly harvested for ornamental purposes. The main medicinal parts of Allium sativum are actually the bulb and leaves, which are used to produce various health benefits.

plant_parts.name: petals
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum petals, also known as the corolla, contain a small amount of flavonoids and volatile oils, contributing to their medicinal properties. However, they are not typically considered a significant source of medicinal compounds compared to other parts of the plant. The flowers' petals are often removed before using garlic in cooking and traditional medicine.

plant_parts.name: stem
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum stem is not commonly used as a medicinal part, but it is believed to have some potential health benefits due to its sulfur content. However, there is limited scientific research on the medicinal properties of the stem. The focus of medicinal use for Allium sativum primarily lies in its bulbs, which are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties.

plant_parts.name: sprouts
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum sprouts are rich in antioxidants and contain compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The sprouts also contain high levels of vitamins C and E, as well as minerals like potassium and manganese, making them a nutritious addition to a healthy diet. In traditional medicine, the sprouts of Allium sativum have been used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.

plant_parts.name: stems
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum stems, while not typically considered a primary medicinal part of the plant, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The stems of Allium sativum are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their potential health benefits. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and applications of Allium sativum stems.

plant_parts.name: powder
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum powder, also known as garlic powder, is used in various medicinal applications due to its antibacterial and antiviral properties. It is often utilized to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of bloating and gas. Additionally, the powder has been traditionally used to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure.

plant_parts.name: sap
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum sap, also known as garlic juice, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The sap has been employed to reduce fever, alleviate colds and flu, and even treat some types of infections. Additionally, the sap's antiseptic and antiviral properties make it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for wounds and skin issues.

plant_parts.name: fiber
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum fiber, also known as garlic fiber, has been found to have potential health benefits due to its high content of soluble and insoluble fiber. The fiber in garlic may help in regulating bowel movements, promoting satiety, and supporting healthy gut bacteria. Furthermore, the dietary fiber in garlic may also contribute to lowering cholesterol levels and managing blood sugar levels.

plant_parts.name: scales
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum scales, which are a part of the medicinal plant commonly known as garlic, have been used in traditional medicine for their antibacterial and antifungal properties. The scales are said to have anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful in treating various health conditions such as colds and respiratory issues. Additionally, the scales of Allium sativum are believed to have antioxidant properties, helping to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress.

plant_parts.name: tubers
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum tubers, commonly known as garlic bulbs, contain compounds with antimicrobial and antiviral properties that have been used to treat various health issues. The tubers of Allium sativum have been traditionally used to reduce blood pressure and lower cholesterol levels due to their high content of flavonoids and saponins. Additionally, the sulfur compounds present in these tubers have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.

plant_parts.name: husk
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum husk, also known as garlic husk, is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery. The husk is also used to treat fever and cough, as well as to support the immune system.

plant_parts.name: rhizome
plant_parts.desc: Allium sativum rhizome, also known as garlic, is a commonly used medicinal part that contains compounds like allicin and diallyl disulfide, which exhibit antibacterial and antifungal properties. The rhizome of Allium sativum has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including high blood pressure, cardiovascular disease, and certain types of cancer.
